title: Weak US Housing Demand Hits Ferguson's Revenue Growth
author: Lekha Gupta
description: Ferguson Enterprises reported Q1 FY26 earnings with sales of $7.50 billion (up 3.6% YoY) and adjusted EPS of $2.28 (up 9.1% YoY), beating estimates. However, weak residential markets declined 1% due to soft new construction and muted repair/remodeling demand, offsetting stronger non-residential growth of 8%. The company maintained its 2026 outlook for low to mid-single digit sales growth and 9.4-9.8% adjusted operating margin.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While Ferguson beat earnings estimates and showed overall revenue growth, the stock declined 2.93% following the announcement. The primary concern is weak residential market demand (down 1%), which represents about half of U.S. revenue. Despite strong non-residential performance, the weakness in the larger residential segment and uncertain economic environment outlook weighed on investor sentiment.

title: Heritage Distribution Holdings Appoints Doug Reichert as Chief Commercial Officer
author: Na
description: Heritage Distribution Holdings, a leading U.S. HVAC/R distributor, announced the appointment of Doug Reichert as Chief Commercial Officer effective immediately. Reichert brings over 20 years of industry experience, most recently as Region President at Carrier Enterprise and previously with Ferguson. He will oversee commercial strategy including pricing, purchasing, marketing, national accounts, and private label operations.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mentioned only as a former employer where Reichert spent over two decades. No direct business impact or strategic information about Ferguson is provided in the article.

title: Step Inside: Third Annual Living by Design Virtual Showhouse Now Open
author: Not Specified
description: The Living by Design Virtual Showhouse, produced by Embello, has launched its third edition featuring 11 top interior designers and 12 sponsoring brands in a fully virtual experience. The digitally-created 3-bedroom home showcases 13 spaces by award-winning designers including Bobby Berk and LUXE Gold List designers. The virtual tour allows consumers to explore designs unconstrained by budget and directly purchase featured products, with a $5,000 upgrade sweepstakes offered to one winner.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Ferguson Home is a Premier sponsor, securing prominent placement and brand exposure in the virtual showhouse experience.

title: Ferguson Q4 Earnings Top Estimates As Non-Residential Strength Offsets Housing Weakness (CORRECTED)
author: Lekha Gupta
description: Ferguson Enterprises reported Q4 FY25 earnings that beat consensus estimates, with adjusted operating profit increasing 13.8% Y/Y to $625 million. While residential revenue declined 2% Y/Y due to weak housing markets, non-residential revenue grew 10% driven by strong execution and capital projects. The company projects 2026 net sales growth in low to mid-single digits with adjusted operating margins of 9.4%-9.8%.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company beat earnings estimates with strong adjusted operating profit growth of 13.8% Y/Y and 70 bps margin expansion. Non-residential segment showed robust 10% revenue growth driven by capital projects and strong execution. Management expressed confidence in outperformance and long-term growth drivers, though residential weakness and modest 2026 guidance temper the overall outlook.

title: Stock Market Today: Nasdaq, S&P 500 Futures Gain As FOMC Begins Its 2-Day Meet — Tesla, Adtran, Ferguson In Focus
author: Rishabh Mishra
description: U.S. stock futures rose ahead of the Federal Reserve's two-day meeting, with investors anticipating potential interest rate cuts. The market showed mixed performance, with Communication Services and Technology sectors leading gains, while the FOMC's decision on Wednesday is a key focus.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock up 2.29% after Elon Musk bought 2.57 million shares through his trust
Announced $6.3 billion agreement with CoreWeave for cloud capacity
Shares rose 7.60% after securing agreement with NVIDIA
Plunged 17.28% after missing quarterly earnings and revenue estimates
Slightly up 1.55% ahead of earnings report
